name: disting mk4
description: Compact multi-algorithm module with 100+ algorithms covering VCO, LFO, quantizer, envelope, delay, reverb, sampler, filter, effects, logic and more. Algorithm selected via encoder menu.

controls.name: S (Encoder)
controls.description: Rotary encoder/push button. Press to enter menu; turn to scroll; press to confirm.
controls.detail: Hold S + turn = select parameter number · menu: Algorithm / Save / Load / Help / Settings / Calibrate

controls.name: Z Knob
controls.description: Algorithm-specific control knob. Also has push-button function for tap-tempo or knob recorder.
controls.detail: Summed with Z CV input · press Z = tap tempo or toggle knob recorder in many algorithms

controls.name: Z CV Input
controls.description: CV input summed with Z knob for real-time control of Z parameter.
controls.detail: Top input jack · LED illuminated (red=positive, blue=negative)

inputs.name: Z CV
inputs.description: Summed with Z knob; controls Z parameter of current algorithm

inputs.name: X
inputs.description: Algorithm-specific input; function depends on selected algorithm

inputs.name: Y
inputs.description: Algorithm-specific input; function depends on selected algorithm

outputs.name: A
outputs.description: Primary output; function depends on selected algorithm

outputs.name: B
outputs.description: Secondary output; function depends on selected algorithm

behaviors.name: Precision Math (A group)
behaviors.description: A1 Precision Adder (X+Y+offset/A, X-Y-offset/B), A2 Four-Quadrant Multiplier (ring mod/VCA), A3 Full-wave Rectifier, A4 Min/Max, A5 Lin/Exp Converter, A6 Quantizer, A7 Comparator, A8 Dual Waveshaper.

behaviors.name: Sample, Slew & Tracking (B group)
behaviors.description: B1 Sample and Hold, B2 Slew Rate Limiter (portamento), B3 Pitch & Envelope Tracker, B4 Clockable Delay/Echo, B5 LFO, B6 Clockable LFO, B7 VCO with linear FM, B8 VCO with waveshaping.

behaviors.name: Delay & Processing (C–D groups)
behaviors.description: C1 Counter, C2 Voltage-Controlled Delay, C3–C4 Clockable Ping Pong Delay, C5 Resonator, C6 Vocoder, C7 Phaser, C8 Bit Crusher; D1 DJ Filter, D2 Tape Delay, D3 Waveform Animator, D4 State Variable Filter, D5–D8 LP/HP/BP/Notch Filters.

behaviors.name: Envelopes & VCA (E–F groups)
behaviors.description: E1 AR Envelope, E2 AR Envelope+VCA, E3 Dual AR, E4–E6 Compressors, E7 Euro-to-Buchla, E8 Buchla-to-Euro; F1–F4 Clockable AD Envelopes (with mute/gate/trigger/VCA), F5–F8 Shift Register Random CVs/Quantized/Triggers.

behaviors.name: Pitch Reference & Clock (G group)
behaviors.description: G1 ES-1 Emulation, G2 ES-2 Emulation, G3–G4 Pitch/Frequency Reference, G5 Tuner, G6 Clock, G7 MIDI/CV, G8 CV/MIDI.

behaviors.name: Dual & Utility (H group)
behaviors.description: H1 Crossfade/Pan, H2 Dual Sample & Hold, H3–H4 Dual Quantizer, H5 Dual Euclidean Patterns, H6 Dual Delayed Pulse Generator, H7 Noise, H8 Quantizer 2.

behaviors.name: Audio Playback & Sampling (I–J groups)
behaviors.description: I1–I7 Audio Playback variants (basic, clocked, V/Oct, Z speed, reverse, scrub, dual); J1–J3 MIDI File Playback, J4–J6 Audio Playback (end CV, recorder, multisample), J7 Mono Audio Recorder, J8 Crossfade.

behaviors.name: Wavetable & SD Delay (K group)
behaviors.description: K1 Wavetable VCO, K2 Clockable Wavetable LFO, K3 Wavetable Waveshaper, K4 Clockable Wavetable Envelope, K5 Programmable Quantizer, K6–K8 Clockable SD Delay variants.

behaviors.name: Reverb, Filter & Chorus (L group)
behaviors.description: L1 Stereo Reverb, L2 Mono-to-Stereo Reverb, L3 Dual Reverb, L4 Dual Vowel Filter, L5 Stereo Chorus, L6 Mono Chorus, L7 Mixer, L8 Gate.

behaviors.name: Logic & Modulation (M–N groups)
behaviors.description: M1 Delayed LFO, M2 Scaled LFO, M3 Logic, M4 Half-wave Rectifier, M5 Stereo Filter, M6 Stereo Tape Delay, M7 Granular Pitch Shifter, M8 Chaos; N1 Switch, N2 Rotary, N3 Attenuverter/Offset, N4 Low Pass Gate, N5 Pulsar VCO, N6 Phase Shifter, N7 Frequency Shifter, N8 Clockable SD Ping Pong.

behaviors.name: Knob Recorder
behaviors.description: Records Z knob movements into a loop (up to ~14 seconds). Release to begin looped playback. Turn Z slightly to regain manual control.

behaviors.name: Tap Tempo
behaviors.description: Pressing Z sets delay/LFO time from interval between presses. Division is factored in for subdivided algorithms.

patchIdeas.name: Quantized random melody
patchIdeas.patch: A6 Quantizer forces a random CV onto a musical scale so a VCO plays in key.
steps: Press the S encoder to open the menu, twist to Algorithm, press S to confirm.
Twist S to A-6 Quantizer, press S to load.
Patch a noise or sample-and-hold CV source OUT into the disting X input.
Patch the disting A output into your VCO 1V/oct input.
Turn the Z knob fully clockwise until the display briefly shows the scale name 'Triad' or 'Pent' (Z positive = scale select).
Patch the disting B output into your envelope trigger input (B fires a 10ms pulse whenever the quantized note changes).
Play a clock or LFO into the X source so the voltage drifts, and listen.
patchIdeas.signalOut: A output — quantized 1V/oct pitch CV; B output — 5V trigger pulse on every note change.
patchIdeas.hear: A VCO stepping through the notes of the selected scale. Rotate Z through scales while it plays to morph from Chromatic to Major to Pentatonic — the melody stays in key as you change.
patchIdeas.diagram:
disting mk4:
* Algorithm: A-6 Quantizer
* Z knob: scale select (positive = scale, negative = trigger mode)

- Noise/S&H (OUT) >> disting mk4 (X) // raw pitch CV
- disting mk4 (A) p> VCO (1V/oct) // quantized pitch
- disting mk4 (B) t> Envelope (Trig) // trigger on note change

patchIdeas.name: Tap-tempo delay/echo
patchIdeas.patch: B4 Clockable Delay/Echo locks delay time to a clock on Y, with Z setting feedback.
steps: Press S, twist to Algorithm, press S, twist to B-4 Clockable Delay/Echo, press S to load.
Patch your audio source (voice, drum, synth) OUT into the disting X input.
Patch a clock or gate output (any rising edge over 1V) into the disting Y input — the time between pulses sets the delay time.
Turn the Z knob to about 2 o'clock for roughly 60–70% feedback.
Patch the disting A output into your mixer or next effect — at default parameter 1 = 0, A carries the wet/dry mix and B carries delay only.
Press and release the Z knob twice in rhythm to tap a tempo if you do not have a clock source.
Adjust Z live to dial feedback from short slap to self-oscillating runaway.
patchIdeas.signalOut: A output — mix of dry signal and delay at audio level; B output — delay-only (wet) tap for parallel routing.
patchIdeas.hear: Rhythmic echoes locked to your clock. Tap Z twice and the delay time jumps to match — push Z past 3 o'clock and the repeats build into a feedback drone.
patchIdeas.diagram:
disting mk4:
* Algorithm: B-4 Clockable Delay/Echo
* Z knob: feedback (press Z = tap tempo)
* Param 1 = 0: A = mix, B = delay only

- Source (OUT) -> disting mk4 (X) // audio in
- Clock (OUT) c> disting mk4 (Y) // sets delay time
- disting mk4 (A) -> Mixer (IN) // wet/dry mix

patchIdeas.name: Clocked wavetable LFO sweep
patchIdeas.patch: K2 Clockable Wavetable LFO scans a wavetable from the SD card, locked to a master clock, to modulate filter cutoff.
steps: Make sure a MicroSD card with wavetables is inserted in the front slot.
Press S, twist to Algorithm, twist to K-2 Clockable Wavetable LFO, press S to load.
Press S again, twist to Parameter, and with Hold-S pick parameter 0 (Chooses the wavetable) — twist to a wavetable number you like.
Patch a clock output (any pulse over 1V) into the disting X input — the cycle time of the LFO follows the clock.
Patch the disting A output into your filter cutoff CV input.
Leave the Y input unpatched for now (Y sweeps through wavetable waveforms; try plugging a slow LFO into Y later).
Turn the Z knob to display '1' (no division), then twist to '-2' to run the LFO at half the clock rate, or '+2' for double.
patchIdeas.signalOut: A output — wavetable LFO CV with attenuverter (param 2); B output — second attenuverted copy (param 3) for a second modulation destination.
patchIdeas.hear: The filter opens and closes on a complex wavetable shape, locked rhythmically to your clock. Turning Z divides or multiplies the rate in whole steps; plug Y into a slow LFO to hear the waveform itself morph.
patchIdeas.diagram:
disting mk4:
* Algorithm: K-2 Clockable Wavetable LFO
* Z knob: integer multiplier/divider of clock
* Param 0: wavetable number

- Clock (OUT) c> disting mk4 (X) // sets LFO cycle time
- disting mk4 (A) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- disting mk4 (B) >> VCA (CV) // second attenuverted copy

patchIdeas.name: Dual Euclidean drum patterns
patchIdeas.patch: H5 Dual Euclidean Patterns turns one clock into two related Euclidean gate trains, with Z setting the second pattern's pulse count.
steps: Press S, twist to Algorithm, twist to H-5 Dual Euclidean Patterns, press S to load.
Patch your master clock output into the disting X input (each rising edge over 1V advances one step).
Hold S and twist to Parameter 0 (Steps), set to 16.
Release, then Hold S and twist to Parameter 1 (Pulses pattern 1), set to 5 — this is output A's density.
Turn the Z knob to set pattern 2's pulse count live (1–16 pulses over 16 steps) — watch the display.
Patch disting A output into your kick drum trigger input.
Patch disting B output into your snare or hat trigger input.
Optional: patch a reset pulse into the Y input to re-sync the pattern to bar 1.
Start the clock and listen.
patchIdeas.signalOut: A output — Euclidean gate train 1 (pulses set by parameter 1); B output — Euclidean gate train 2 (pulses set live by Z).
patchIdeas.hear: Two interlocking rhythmic pulse patterns from one clock. Rotate Z while it plays to shift the second pattern from sparse (1) to dense (16) hits without stopping the groove.
patchIdeas.diagram:
disting mk4:
* Algorithm: H-5 Dual Euclidean Patterns
* Z knob: pulses for pattern 2 (B)
* Param 0 = 16 (steps), Param 1 = 5 (pulses pattern 1)

- Clock (OUT) c> disting mk4 (X) // advances pattern
- Reset (OUT) t> disting mk4 (Y) // optional reset
- disting mk4 (A) t> Kick (Trig) // pattern 1
- disting mk4 (B) t> Snare (Trig) // pattern 2
